type_error_policy: Pycairo runtime TypeError assertions map to MoonBit compile-time rejection where the public signature carries the distinction. Device is an abstract opaque owner, path and stream construction are separate typed constructors, and script mode/comment arguments are statically typed. Cairo only exposes recording and image surfaces through one surface pointer type, so script replay additionally performs a checked runtime surface-kind validation and raises CairoError(SurfaceTypeMismatch, _).

static_api: pub fn[T] Device::with_finished(Self, () -> T raise CairoError) -> T raise CairoError
test_context_manager.adaptation: MoonBit has no Python context-manager protocol; with_finished provides the same finish-on-scope-exit behavior for successful and Cairo-error paths.

test_cmp_hash.adaptation: Opaque Device values compare and hash by the referenced cairo_device_t identity; heterogeneous Python object comparisons are unavailable statically.

static_api: pub fn Surface::get_device(Self) -> Device? raise CairoError

test_script_device.adaptation: Pycairo overloads path strings and binary file-like objects in ScriptDevice. MoonBit uses separate path and stream-callback constructors; missing, null, text-stream, and unrelated arguments are rejected statically, while embedded NUL bytes raise CairoInvalidArgument(InvalidString, _). Both constructors return the single opaque Device owner, whose backend is observable through get_type.

test_from_recording_surface.adaptation: Null and unrelated values are rejected statically. Cairo surface backends share one opaque Surface type, so non-recording surfaces are rejected by a checked C boundary with CairoError(SurfaceTypeMismatch, _); finished devices raise CairoError(DeviceFinished, _).
